name: docopt (CLI from Docstring)

performance.notes: Parse docstring at startup; negligible overhead

agent_readiness.notes: CLI argument parsing from docstring. """Usage: prog [--count=N] <file>. Options: --count=N Number [default: 10].""" from docopt import docopt. args = docopt(__doc__). args['--count'] → '10'. args['<file>'] → positional. No decorators, no builder pattern — just a docstring. Supports: positional args, options with values, flags, multiple same option, subcommands. args = docopt(__doc__, argv=['--count=5', 'file.txt']). docopt.magic_docopt for type annotations. Minimal: 68 lines of source code. Widely ported to other languages.

gotchas: Original docopt is not actively maintained — consider docopt-ng fork
All values are strings — manual type conversion required
Error messages show raw docstring — less friendly than Click
Complex nested subcommands are verbose in docstring format
best_when: Simple CLI tools where the docstring IS the documentation
Scripts where CLI definition should match the help text exactly
When minimal dependencies are required (single file, 68 lines)
Quick CLI prototyping without decorators or builders
avoid_when: Complex CLIs with many options and subcommands (use Click or Typer)
Type-safe CLIs with validation (use Typer + Pydantic)
Large applications needing testing utilities and auto-completion
summary: docopt generates CLI from a docstring. docopt(__doc__) → args dict. Usage patterns in docstring define interface. Zero decorators. 68-line single-file library. args['--count'], args['<file>']. MIT by Vladimir Keleshev. Best for minimal simple CLIs where docstring IS the help. Original not maintained — use docopt-ng. All values are strings. No type annotations. Complex CLIs prefer Click/Typer.
